Recommended Action: Select vendor, menu, order time, or delivery option that improves satisfaction and reduces congestion
Target Variable: Vendor choice, menu choice, order timing, queue choice
Optimization Objective: Maximize satisfaction, nutrition, affordability, and convenience while reducing waiting time
Expected Benefit: Shorter queue, better nutrition, higher satisfaction, better demand distribution
Implementation Cost / Burden: Small behavioral adjustment; possible preference compromise
Acceptance Factors: Trust in recommendation, menu preference, price sensitivity, distance, habit, perceived benefit
Monitoring Variables: Waiting time, satisfaction score, nutrition score, affordability score, choice acceptance rate
Example Recommendation: Choose Vendor_B at 12:15 because queue is shorter and nutrition score is acceptable

Recommended Action: Adjust production quantity, menu mix, price promotion, staffing, and preparation timing
Target Variable: Production quantity Q_v(t), mission intensity M_v(t), menu mix, staffing level
Optimization Objective: Maximize demand fulfillment, profit, food quality, and utilization while minimizing waste
Expected Benefit: Higher sales, lower leftover, lower shortage, better capacity utilization
Implementation Cost / Burden: Ingredient cost, labor adjustment, menu preparation complexity, autonomy cost
Acceptance Factors: Expected profit, trust in MSRS, risk tolerance, ingredient availability, labor availability
Monitoring Variables: Meals produced, meals served, leftover, waste, profit, vendor utilization, recommendation acceptance
Example Recommendation: Produce 165 meals and increase healthy-menu share by 10% during exam week

Recommended Action: Adjust ingredient delivery quantity, delivery timing, stock allocation, and sourcing plan
Target Variable: Delivery quantity, delivery time, stock allocation, supply mission intensity M_s(t)
Optimization Objective: Maximize supply reliability and inventory turnover while minimizing stockout and spoilage
Expected Benefit: Reduced supplier bottleneck, improved vendor readiness, lower spoilage, better delivery utilization
Implementation Cost / Burden: Logistics cost, early delivery cost, inventory holding cost, routing adjustment
Acceptance Factors: Delivery feasibility, expected revenue, logistics capacity, stock availability, relationship trust
Monitoring Variables: Delivery reliability, stockout rate, supplier utilization, spoilage, delivery delay
Example Recommendation: Advance vegetable delivery by one time step and increase quantity by 15% before lunch surge

Recommended Action: Assign delivery routes, adjust delivery capacity, batch orders, or redirect riders to high-demand zones
Target Variable: Route assignment, delivery batch size, delivery timing, delivery mission intensity M_d(t)
Optimization Objective: Minimize delivery delay and idle time while maximizing coverage and service reliability
Expected Benefit: Lower delivery time, better order fulfillment, reduced rider idle time, improved online service quality
Implementation Cost / Burden: Route change cost, fuel or energy cost, rider workload, coordination burden
Acceptance Factors: Route feasibility, rider availability, expected fee, traffic condition, delivery platform trust
Monitoring Variables: Delivery time, completed deliveries, route efficiency, rider utilization, delivery satisfaction
Example Recommendation: Allocate two additional riders to the north campus route during lunch peak

Recommended Action: Adjust subsidy, vendor allocation, nutrition target, sustainability rule, or facility support
Target Variable: Subsidy level, policy constraint, facility allocation, EVI weights, nutrition target
Optimization Objective: Maximize student welfare, mission achievement, affordability, sustainability, and vendor viability
Expected Benefit: Improved institutional mission, better affordability, healthier meals, reduced ecosystem risk
Implementation Cost / Burden: Budget cost, administrative complexity, fairness concerns, policy approval time
Acceptance Factors: Budget availability, institutional priority, evidence quality, stakeholder fairness, regulatory alignment
Monitoring Variables: Mission achievement, affordability, student satisfaction, vendor viability, sustainability score, EVI
Example Recommendation: Increase temporary meal subsidy by 10% under inflation scenario to preserve affordability

Recommended Action: Select MSRS policy, adjust model parameters, run scenario tests, update dashboard, or trigger alerts
Target Variable: Policy π_k, scenario set, EVI weights, recommendation frequency, model parameters
Optimization Objective: Improve recommendation quality, robustness, transparency, and learning across scenarios
Expected Benefit: Better coordination, stronger evidence, improved experiment reproducibility, faster response to disruption
Implementation Cost / Burden: Compute cost, model maintenance, data validation effort, dashboard complexity
Acceptance Factors: Data quality, model confidence, stakeholder trust, computational feasibility, governance approval
Monitoring Variables: Prediction error, policy rank, robustness index, recommendation acceptance, dashboard usage
Example Recommendation: Run multi-shock scenario and switch from cost-first to balanced-EVI policy

Recommended Action: Adjust inspection priority, compliance threshold, audit frequency, or safety-warning trigger
Target Variable: Inspection intensity, compliance threshold, audit priority, penalty level
Optimization Objective: Maximize safety, fairness, compliance, trust, and environmental accountability
Expected Benefit: Reduced safety risk, improved trust, better compliance, lower institutional risk
Implementation Cost / Burden: Inspection cost, enforcement workload, administrative burden, stakeholder resistance
Acceptance Factors: Legal authority, risk evidence, audit capacity, institutional support, urgency of incident
Monitoring Variables: Compliance rate, safety incidents, audit findings, environmental violations, trust score
Example Recommendation: Prioritize inspection for vendors with repeated waste and hygiene anomalies

Recommended Action: Synchronize stakeholder actions across demand, production, supply, delivery, governance, and regulation
Target Variable: M_i(t), Q_i(t), A_i(t), RMOV_i(t), PSKVE flows, EVI(t)
Optimization Objective: Maximize ecosystem value subject to stakeholder constraints, mission goals, and scenario conditions
Expected Benefit: Higher mission achievement, improved sustainability, better resilience, reduced systemic inefficiency
Implementation Cost / Burden: Coordination cost, data-sharing cost, autonomy cost, computational cost
Acceptance Factors: Trust, perceived fairness, expected benefit, implementation cost, governance legitimacy
Monitoring Variables: EVI, mission synchronization index, acceptance rate, waste reduction, resilience, satisfaction, profit
Example Recommendation: Students shift demand, vendors adjust production, suppliers advance delivery, university activates subsidy
